title: rootless: skip check fo /etc/containers/registries.conf
body:
the warning can be confusing when used in rootless mode as the
unprivileged user has no way for setting it up.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2955

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Do we have a local version of the file? If not, I think it might still make sense to retain it, because a lot of functionality seems to break without having registries defined
ideally we would never NOT have that file ...
Well you would NOT have the file if the podman was installed/built locally in your homedir.  For the most part users seeing this are probably on distributions that did not ship a registries.conf

> Do we have a local version of the file? If not, I think it might still make sense to retain it, because a lot of functionality seems to break without having registries defined

yes, I've amended the code to also look for the user conf file.
